user.title: HA-MCP Custom Component
user.description: Choose what to add. **HA MCP Tools** enables the privileged file and YAML configuration services used by ha-mcp. **HA-MCP Server** runs the full ha-mcp server inside Home Assistant and exposes it remotely through a webhook. You can add both.

tools.title: HA MCP Tools
tools.description: This entry provides the advanced file and YAML configuration services used by ha-mcp. No Home Assistant Supervisor was detected, so the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on can't be installed automatically here — run ha-mcp via Docker or pip instead. Select **Submit** to enable the services.

addon.title: Install the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on
addon.description: Home Assistant Supervisor was detected. This integration can add the add-on repository and install and start the **Home Assistant MCP Server** add-on for you — no need to add a repository URL by hand. Leave the box checked to install it now, or uncheck to set up only the integration services.

data.install_addon: Install the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on

install_failed.title: Add-on installation failed

server.title: HA-MCP Server
server.description: This runs the full ha-mcp server inside Home Assistant and exposes it remotely through a Home Assistant webhook (reachable via Nabu Casa or any reverse proxy). Select **Submit** to start it; you can change the port, binding, and authentication afterward in the integration options.

progress.install_addon: Adding the add-on repository, installing the Home Assistant MCP Server add-on, and starting it. This can take a few minutes.

abort.already_configured: This entry is already set up.

abort.no_options: The HA MCP Tools services entry has no options to configure.

init.title: HA-MCP Server
init.description:
Configure the HA-MCP server. Open the [HA-MCP settings panel](/ha-mcp) for tool management and server settings. Changes here are applied on save.

{connect_url}

data_description.channel: Stable is the pinned, tested release. Development installs the newest build and refreshes it on every reload or restart. A developer package override below takes precedence over this.
data_description.server_port: The local port the MCP server itself listens on (default 9584). This is NOT your Home Assistant port (8123), and it stays clear of the MCP Server add-on's 9583 so both can run.
data_description.bind_host: Who can reach the MCP server port directly. "This machine only" keeps it loopback-only; the webhook and the sidebar panel keep working either way.
data_description.webhook_auth: How MCP clients prove themselves at the webhook URL. With the secret URL, the link itself is the credential. With Home Assistant sign-in, clients such as claude.ai log in with a Home Assistant administrator account (OAuth).
data_description.pip_spec: Leave empty. Only for testing a specific ha-mcp build (for example a pre-release pin); overrides the release channel until cleared.
data_description.server_url: The URL the in-process server uses to reach your Home Assistant (usually this instance itself). Leave the default unless you know you need a different route.

server_start_failed.title: The HA-MCP in-process server failed to start

server_package_install_failed.title: The HA-MCP in-process server package could not be installed
